title: TLS Cipher Suites
description: Named combinations of cryptographic algorithms used for key exchange, authentication, encryption, and integrity in TLS.
fullExplanation: A cipher suite specifies the complete set of algorithms used in a TLS session. In TLS 1.2, a suite name like TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 means: key exchange=ECDHE, authentication=RSA, encryption=AES-256-GCM, MAC=SHA384. TLS 1.3 simplified this — only 5 cipher suites exist, all using AEAD encryption, and key exchange is always ECDHE or DHE (forward secrecy is mandatory).
keyFacts: Forward secrecy: ECDHE/DHE key exchange — compromise of private key doesn't decrypt past sessions
AEAD (Authenticated Encryption with Associated Data): encryption + integrity in one operation
AES-GCM: hardware-accelerated on modern CPUs (AES-NI instruction set)
ChaCha20-Poly1305: software-efficient — faster than AES on devices without AES-NI
TLS 1.3 removed: RSA key exchange, CBC mode, MD5, SHA-1, RC4, DES, 3DES
NULL cipher: no encryption — used for testing only, never in production
openssl ciphers -v: list all supported cipher suites
